instructions: ULC defines an open JSON Schema standard for lighting datasheets. Focus on cross-file consistency and standard correctness, not line-level nits. Prioritize: (1) JSON Schema correctness in schema/** — required vs optional, enums/patterns, $ref, additionalProperties, breaking vs additive; (2) Go validator semantics in tools/** — error handling, nil-safety, version pinning, exit codes, parity with what schema/** documents; (3) consistency across schema/**, mappings/**, templates/**, docs/** — a field rename should propagate everywhere; (4) versioning — SemVer impact, CHANGELOG accuracy; (5) provenance, dual-unit SI-authoritative values, metadata-only constraint per CONTRIBUTING.md and GOVERNANCE.md. Skip examples/** — real-cutsheet fixtures. Avoid Markdown style or typo comments.

rules.rule: Flag breaking JSON Schema changes: removed field, tightened type, narrowed enum, removed enum value, changed required[], added required field, changed $id/$ref. Breaking changes need a major bump per SemVer.
Field added/renamed/removed in schema/** must propagate to mappings/** and templates/**. Inconsistency is a defect.
tools/** is the reference implementation. Flag where validator behavior diverges from schema/**.
Wrap errors with %w; no panics in library paths; non-zero exit on validation failure. Flag swallowed errors and bare panics.
Unit-bearing fields use the dual-unit SI-authoritative shape. Flag single-unit additions or swapped authority.
Example/template values must come from real cutsheets. Flag fabricated photometric, electrical, or regulatory values.
Any schema/** change should have a CHANGELOG.md entry under the active version, with breaking changes called out.
ULC stores regulatory data as metadata only (links, ids, dates). Flag fields that copy normative third-party standard text.

files.description: Contribution rules; metadata-only and example-sourcing constraints.
Versioned history; check schema-affecting PRs have an entry.
Canonical JSON Schema for a ULC record. Source of truth.
